draft.rationale: To resolve the collision and curvature violations on the closing loop connection (M19), we route the spline clear of the existing pipe structure. The primary collision was caused by the path cutting diagonally through the first triangular peak (M3-M6, located in X: 59-101, Z: -2-39). By extending the spline horizontally to the left (X = -100 mm) at the high elevation of the start port (Z = 178.923 mm), we completely bypass all existing peaks. We then descend vertically well to the left of the origin (at X = -100 mm) down to Z = -173.2 mm, which aligns perfectly with the target port's incoming tangent vector [0.5, 0.0, 0.866]. This creates a smooth, generous U-turn that comfortably exceeds the minimum 20.0 mm bend radius requirement without colliding with any part of the assembly.
